name: Legal
description: Review contracts, audit privacy compliance, prep Delaware filings, run trademark searches, and answer legal questions on the spot. Drafts NDAs, policies, and escalation briefs - never files, signs, or sends anything on your behalf.
author: Houston

agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "weekly-legal-review", "name": "Monday legal review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped last week, what's pending signature, what's due in the next 3 deadlines, and any attorney-review backlog — rolled up across contracts, compliance, entity, IP, and advisory.", "prompt": "Run the Monday legal review. Use the track-deadlines-and-signatures skill with scope=weekly-review.

Aggregate everything this agent produced last week from outputs.json — contract reviews, NDA traffic-lights, drafts, privacy audits, filings, advice memos — grouped by domain (Contracts, Compliance, Entity, IP, Advisory). Surface pending signatures from the most recent signature-status/ entry. Pull the next 3 deadlines from deadline-calendar.json (with their cited authority). Surface any attorneyReviewRequired: true entries without a matching escalation-brief in escalations/.

Write the review to weekly-reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — I want you to see the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-deadline-pulse", "name": "Weekly deadline pulse", "description": "Every Thursday afternoon: what legal deadlines are hitting in the next 30 days — Delaware, 83(b), 409A, DSR clocks, TM deadlines, contract renewals. So nothing surprises you Monday.", "prompt": "Run the weekly deadline pulse. Use the track-deadlines-and-signatures skill with scope=deadlines.

Refresh deadline-calendar.json from the canonical set (Delaware March 1, 83(b) 30-day, 409A 12-month, DSR 30/45-day, TM office action 6-month, annual board consent) + enrich with dynamic renewal clocks from counterparty-tracker.json (every open row: renewalDate = effectiveDate + term; notice-deadline = renewalDate - noticePeriod).

Write a 30-day outlook to deadline-summaries/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. For each deadline ≤ 30 days, cite the authority (8 Del. C. §503, IRC §83(b), GDPR Art. 15, contract governing-law, etc.). Always surface — a quiet month is also news.

Log in outputs.json.", "schedule": "0 15 * * 4", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-privacy-audit", "name": "Monthly privacy posture audit", "description": "On the 1st of every month: scrapes the landing page + product and diffs against the deployed Privacy Policy. Silent unless something drifted.", "prompt": "Run the monthly privacy posture audit. Use the audit-compliance skill with scope=privacy-posture.

Scrape the landing page + product routes from universal.company.website (or domains.compliance.landingPageUrl) via Firecrawl. Capture analytics tags, cookies, forms + fields, third-party embeds, subprocessor-revealing scripts. Fetch the deployed Privacy Policy from domains.compliance.deployedPolicies.privacyPolicyUrl. Diff and tag each finding (critical / high / medium / low) with authority citations on criticals.

Compare against the most recent privacy-audits/ entry so you can flag *new* drift vs. findings already reported. Write to privacy-audits/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.

If no new critical or high findings since the last audit (no new undisclosed analytics, no missed subprocessor, no purpose drift beyond prior), end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface only when new drift appeared. On the first run (no prior audit exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-subprocessor-sweep", "name": "Monthly subprocessor sweep", "description": "Mid-month sweep of your integrations + landing for new subprocessors. Silent unless a new vendor appeared without DPA.", "prompt": "Run the monthly subprocessor sweep. Use the audit-compliance skill with scope=subprocessors.

Walk the installed Composio connections and scrape the landing page for vendor clues (Stripe, Intercom, Segment, HotJar, Calendly, etc.). Capture role + dataCategories + transferMechanism + dpaStatus + publicDpaUrl per vendor. Read-merge-write subprocessor-inventory.json. Delta vs prior inventory = added / removed / changed / unchanged. Write subprocessor-reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.

If the delta is empty OR only contains unchanged rows (no new vendors, no DPA gap, no transfer-mechanism shift), end with ROUTINE_OK. Surface on any new vendor or any DPA status flip.
